presentation.chiefComplaint: Bad headache, wants something for it

history.hpi: She describes a severe headache that came on completely out of nowhere about an hour ago while she was sitting — 'like being hit on the back of the head,' the worst she has ever had, peaking instantly. She vomited once and the light bothers her eyes; her neck feels stiff. She is now sitting calmly and asks for a painkiller, assuming it is a migraine.
redFlags: Thunderclap headache: sudden onset peaking within seconds, worst of life
Patient states this headache is different from her usual migraines
Subarachnoid hemorrhage needs urgent non-contrast CT (time-critical)

redFlagConcepts.flag: Thunderclap headache: sudden onset peaking within seconds, worst of life
Patient states this headache is different from her usual migraines
Subarachnoid hemorrhage needs urgent non-contrast CT (time-critical)

expert.esiRationale: The trap: a known migraineur asking for a painkiller, sitting calmly with normal vitals, is the textbook under-triage to ESI 3. But a sudden-onset, instantly-peaking, worst-of-life ('thunderclap') headache with neck stiffness and photophobia, that the patient says is different from her usual migraines, is a subarachnoid hemorrhage until a CT says otherwise — high-risk and time-critical. ESI 2. Vitals are near-normal aside from a high pain score and mild hypertension; the danger is entirely in the history.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored calibration-trap case
